summary: Added protocol template archive/restore state so Home can hide templates without deleting their steps, schedule metadata, active runs, or run history.

contract_status_changes.before: HouseholdProtocol templates had no archive state, so hiding a reusable protocol required either leaving it visible or deleting it.
contract_status_changes.after: HouseholdProtocol stores `isArchived` with legacy decode defaulting to false. HomeStore can archive/unarchive templates, active protocol lists hide archived templates, archived templates reject new runs, and pre-existing active runs can still resume and terminate normally.

contract_status_changes.before: Runtime schedule notification planning consumed all Home protocol templates.
contract_status_changes.after: Runtime schedule notification planning consumes `HomeStore.activeProtocols`, so archived templates are not handed to `ProtocolScheduleNotificationRules` as notification candidates.

residual_risks: No running-app, flow, or screenshot proof was captured for the new Archive/Restore UI affordances.

The notification exclusion is covered by HomeStore active/archived filtering and RootTab wiring inspection, but there is no dedicated end-to-end notification scheduling test for archived protocols in this slice.
recommended_next_reason: The adjacent queued Home lifecycle slice can build on the same protocol-run surface by adding a safe way to revert completed or skipped protocol steps to pending.
